content.problemStatement:
Chef is not feeling well today. He measured his body temperature using a thermometer and it came out to be XXX ¬įF.

A person is said to have fever if his body temperature is **strictly greater** than 989898 ¬įF.

Determine if Chef has fever or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains one integer XXX - the body temperature of Chef in ¬įF.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if Chef has fever. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for example, `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Since X=98X = 98X=98 is not greater than 989898, Chef does not have fever.
Test Case 2: Since X=100X = 100X=100 is greater than 989898, Chef has fever.
Test Case 3: Since X=96X = 96X=96 is not greater than 989898, Chef does not have fever.
